# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 93

This is turn 93. Turns 1 through 92 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 93.

## Turn 93 — image-stratified induction removes all terminal subtraction bookkeeping

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. In the proof, the displayed exponent denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its verifier residue. 

Turn 92 classified not only epimorphisms in the terminal scalar branch, but every possible fixed-frame image.  The correct way to insert that result into the Turn-89 fixed-frame proof is to strengthen the induction from a surjection count to an **exact-image stratification**.

### 1. The universal image-partition identity

Let \((Y,L_Y,\theta)\) be a decorated marked target with fixed tame quotient
\[
H=Y/L_Y
\]
and fixed tame epimorphism
\[
\alpha:T_{\mathrm{tame}}\twoheadrightarrow H.
\]

Suppose
\[
\pi:Y\twoheadrightarrow B
\]
is one of the quotient maps used in a lifting step, with kernel contained in \(L_Y\). Let
\[
\mathcal I_\pi(Y)=\{J\leq Y:\pi(J)=B\}.
\]

For either source
\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_A,G_{\mathbb Q_2}\},
\]
let \(\operatorname{Lift}_\Gamma(Y/B)\) denote the relation-compatible decorated lifts to \(Y\) of fixed-frame surjections to \(B\).

Every such lift has a unique image \(J\in\mathcal I_\pi(Y)\), so there is a disjoint decomposition
\[
\operatorname{Lift}_\Gamma(Y/B)
=
\bigsqcup_{J\in\mathcal I_\pi(Y)}
\operatorname{Sur}_{\alpha,\theta|_J=\psi}(\Gamma,J).
\tag{93.1}
\]

Consequently,
\[
\begin{aligned}
\#\operatorname{Sur}_{\alpha,\theta=\psi}(\Gamma,Y)
={}&
\#\operatorname{Lift}_\Gamma(Y/B)\\
&-
\sum_{\substack{J<Y\\ \pi(J)=B}}
\#\operatorname{Sur}_{\alpha,\theta|_J=\psi}(\Gamma,J).
\end{aligned}
\tag{93.2}
\]

This is ordinary partition by image, not Möbius inversion.

Every \(J\in\mathcal I_\pi(Y)\) still projects onto \(H\). Hence
\[
J/(J\cap L_Y)\cong H.
\]
If \(J<Y\), then
\[
|J\cap L_Y|
=\frac{|J|}{|H|}
<
\frac{|Y|}{|H|}
=|L_Y|.
\tag{93.3}
\]

Thus every proper-image term in (93.2) has a strictly smaller marked wild kernel. Strong induction on \(|L_Y|\) is therefore genuinely well-founded.

### 2. The terminal branch is closed under every proper image

Suppose all chief factors inside \(L_Y\) are scalar. Turns 90–92 give the canonical form
\[
Y\cong H\times_C Q,
\qquad C=H_2,
\]
where \(Q\) is a finite \(2\)-group and the kernels of
\[
H\twoheadrightarrow C,\qquad Q\twoheadrightarrow C
\]
have coprime orders.

Projection to \(Q\) identifies the entire fixed-frame image poset:
\[
\{J\leq Y:\operatorname{pr}_H(J)=H\}
\longleftrightarrow
\{Q'\leq Q:q(Q')=C\},
\tag{93.4}
\]
via
\[
Q'\longmapsto J(Q')=H\times_C Q'.
\tag{93.5}
\]

Let
\[
\Pi=
\left\langle \sigma,x_0,x_1\ \middle|\
x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}
\]
be the common marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient, and let
\[
\alpha_2:\Pi\twoheadrightarrow C
\]
be induced by the fixed tame frame. Since every scalar decoration kills the odd pullback kernel, it factors through a map
\[
\bar\theta:Q\to E.
\]

For every compatible \(Q'\leq Q\), there is an exact bijection
\[
\begin{aligned}
&\{f:\Gamma\to Y:
f_{\mathrm{tame}}=\alpha,\ \theta f=\psi,\
\operatorname{im}f=H\times_C Q'\}\\
&\qquad\cong
\{\beta\in\operatorname{Sur}(\Pi,Q'):
(q|_{Q'})\beta=\alpha_2,\
(\bar\theta|_{Q'})\beta=\psi\}.
\end{aligned}
\tag{93.6}
\]

The right-hand side is literally the same for \(\Gamma_A\) and \(G_{\mathbb Q_2}\). Therefore every exact-image stratum in a terminal target agrees, simultaneously for all proper compatible subgroups \(Q'\).

This is the key insertion into the induction: when a proper image occurring in (93.2) enters the terminal scalar branch, its contribution is not obtained by a further abstract subtraction. It is already given exactly by (93.6).

### 3. The nonterminal critical-block step now has a clean recursion

For a nonterminal target \(G\), retain the Turn-89 critical block
\[
R=\Phi(K)\triangleleft K,
\qquad
M=K/R,
\]
where \(R\) is central elementary abelian and \(M\) has a single nontrivial simple head.

The proof has two lift stages.

#### Elementary \(M\)-stage

Consider
\[
G/R\twoheadrightarrow G/K.
\]

Finite-Heisenberg deformation duality gives equal total lift counts for the candidate and the local group:
\[
\#\operatorname{Lift}_{\Gamma_A}((G/R)/(G/K))
=
\#\operatorname{Lift}_{G_{\mathbb Q_2}}((G/R)/(G/K)).
\tag{93.7}
\]

To pass to surjections onto \(G/R\), apply (93.2). Every proper image \(J<G/R\) mapping onto \(G/K\) has a strictly smaller marked wild kernel by (93.3). Its exact-image count is therefore already known:

- by the inductive theorem if \(J\) remains nonterminal;
- directly from (93.6) if \(J\) is terminal scalar.

Hence the surjection counts to \(G/R\) agree.

#### Central \(R\)-stage

For
\[
G\twoheadrightarrow G/R,
\]
the Fourier reconstruction, simple-head determinant lemma, finite-Heisenberg duality, and Wall–Arf/local-Kummer comparison give the same number of unobstructed lower surjections and the same \(R\)-lift multiplicity.

No image subtraction is needed at this stage. If a lift has image \(J\) mapping onto \(G/R\), then
\[
(J\cap K)R=K.
\]
Since
\[
R=\Phi(K),
\]
the Frattini property forces
\[
J\cap K=K,
\]
and therefore \(J=G\). Every unobstructed \(R\)-lift is automatically surjective.

Thus the critical-block recurrence is now completely explicit:

1. compare total \(M\)-lifts;
2. subtract exact proper-image strata using strong induction, with the terminal strata supplied directly by the common pro-\(2\) quotient;
3. perform the \(R\)-lift, where Frattini surjectivity eliminates all proper images.

### 4. Strengthened fixed-frame theorem

The induction proves more than equality of the final epimorphism counts.

For every finite decorated marked target \((G,L,\theta)\), every fixed tame frame \(\alpha\), and every subgroup \(J\leq G\) projecting onto \(G/L\), the two sources have equal numbers of decorated fixed-frame maps whose image is exactly \(J\):
\[
\#\{f:\Gamma_A\to G:\operatorname{im}f=J,\ f_{\mathrm{tame}}=\alpha,\theta f=\psi\}
\]
\[
=
\#\{f:G_{\mathbb Q_2}\to G:\operatorname{im}f=J,\ f_{\mathrm{tame}}=\alpha,\theta f=\psi\}.
\tag{93.8}
\]

In the terminal branch, this is the literal bijection (93.6). In the nonterminal branch, it follows recursively from the two-stage critical-block lift.

Taking \(J=G\), summing over tame frames, removing the decoration, and specializing to
\[
L=O_2(G)
\]
recovers the official verifier count.

**ESTABLISHED**: Inserted the Turn-92 terminal image-poset theorem into the fixed-tame-frame proof as an exact-image induction. Proper-image correction is now the disjoint identity (93.2), well-founded because every proper fixed-frame image has strictly smaller marked wild kernel. Terminal proper images are handled directly and simultaneously by compatible subgroups of the common finite \(2\)-group factor, while the final central Frattini lift admits no proper images at all.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The nonterminal theorem still depends on the ordinary Thompson critical-subgroup input and the finite-Heisenberg and Wall–Arf/local-Kummer propositions consolidated in Turn 89.

**NEXT**: Replace the terminal paragraph and all informal “subtract proper images” clauses in the standalone proof by (93.1)–(93.8), yielding a single image-stratified fixed-frame theorem ready for the unchanged verifier submission.
